rules_specification_check.notes: The canonical rules document is comprehensive and well-structured. It defines all required rule sections: match configuration, deployment rules, turn lifecycle, attack resolution, suit effects, destruction rules, reinforcement rules, collapse behavior, pass logic, and win conditions. Rules are deterministic and precisely defined.

mapped_rules.engine_component: combat.ts - resolveColumnOverflow() with suit logic (Diamond shield, Club double, Heart shield, Spade double)
combat.ts - isAce() and absorbDamage() with ace invulnerability logic
turns.ts - checkVictory() with pass state tracking
turns.ts - checkVictory() for LP depletion, card depletion, pass limits

rule_engine_traceability.notes: Every significant rule section has clear traceability to engine components. The architecture documentation in docs/system/ARCHITECTURE.md also maps packages to their responsibilities.

event_docs.description: Defines event structure with id, parentId, type, name, timestamp, payload, status
Deterministic replay with stateHashBefore and stateHashAfter
Canonical serialization with alphabetically sorted JSON keys

event_model_verification.notes: The event model is thoroughly documented in RULES.md with clear explanations of the hierarchical structure, event types, and deterministic replay guarantees. The implementation matches the specification in shared/src/schema.ts.

architecture_docs_check.notes: Architecture documentation is comprehensive and explains engine lifecycle, match state transitions, server authority, client interaction model, and deterministic processing. The mermaid diagram visualizes the architecture well. Minor improvement: could include more detail on OpenTelemetry integration.

documentation_drift.notes: No significant drift detected. The example in documentation-review.md about face cards having value 11 is actually consistent - J/Q/K all have value 11 as defined in RANK_VALUES in shared/src/schema.ts. The docs/review/documentation-review.md file is itself a test example showing what drift would look like, not actual drift.

terms_detected.definition: Present in RULES.md - the game system name
docs/RULES.md Section 5 - card placement before combat
docs/RULES.md Section 13 - column shift after card destruction
docs/RULES.md Section 2.2 - vertical stack of cards
docs/RULES.md Section 2.2 - position index inside column (0=front)
docs/RULES.md Section 2.2 - remaining damage after resolving target

terminology_verification.notes: All key domain terms are defined in the canonical vocabulary section (Section 2) of RULES.md and used consistently throughout the documentation.

missing_topics: Rule modification process documentation - could be more explicit about how to modify rules and propagate changes
Testing strategy documentation - while tests exist, a dedicated test strategy doc would help contributors
developer_docs_check.notes: Developer documentation is solid but could be improved with more explicit guidance on rule modification workflow and testing strategy.

missing_explanations: Player-facing quick-start guide - new players would benefit from a simplified explanation before diving into the technical spec
Visual examples/diagrams of phalanx formations and combat flow
gameplay_docs_check.notes: RULES.md serves as the authoritative gameplay documentation but is technical and dense. A separate player-facing tutorial or quick-start guide would improve onboarding for new players. The technical depth is excellent for verification purposes.

documentation_strengths: Comprehensive canonical rules specification in docs/RULES.md with deterministic definitions
Excellent rule-to-engine traceability - every rule section maps to implementation
Well-documented event model with OpenTelemetry-inspired hierarchical structure
Clear architecture documentation with visual diagrams
CI enforcement of rules consistency via pnpm rules:check
recommended_improvements: Add player-facing quick-start guide for new players
Add visual diagrams for gameplay mechanics (phalanx formations, combat flow)
Add explicit rule modification workflow documentation for contributors
Add a glossary section in RULES.md for quick term reference
